summary: BTC Tools | Multicast Mapping | Adding Multicast Mapping Saves the Details Correctly
gherkin:
When the user clicks on the "Add Multicast Mapping" button
And the user enters a Call Sign into the "Call Sign" input field (Example: "ABC", "ARID")
And the user clicks on the "Priority" dropdown and selects an option (Example: "PRIMARY")
And the user enters a Multicast Address in the input field, using the format udp://: (Example: "rtp://239.130.25.1:8000")
And the user clicks on the "Source Location" dropdown and selects an option (Example: "SCS1")
And the user clicks on the "Submit" button
Then the user should see a new row in the Multicast Mapping table containing the entered values

steps.action:
*When* the user clicks on the "Add Multicast Mapping" button

*And* the user enters a Call Sign into the "Call Sign" input field (Example: "ABC", "ARID")

*And* the user clicks on the "Priority" dropdown and selects an option (Example: "PRIMARY")
*And* the user enters a Multicast Address in the input field, using the format udp://: (Example: "rtp://239.130.25.1:8000")

*And* the user clicks on the "Source Location" dropdown and selects an option (Example: "SCS1")

*And* the user clicks on the "Submit" button
steps.result: *Then* the user should see a new row in the Multicast Mapping table containing the entered values
